Title: Optimized paired-sgRNA/Cas9 cloning and expression cassette triggers high-efficiency multiplex genome editing in kiwifruit

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Carotenoid biosynthetic enzyme; phytoene desaturase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces desaturation into phytoene in the carotenoid biosynthetic pathway, converting phytoene toward ζ-carotene intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic pigments and precursors related to normal green pigmentation; loss causes albino phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AcPDS function induces albino leaves/plantlets in kiwifruit.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcPDS was identified as the kiwifruit homolog of phytoene desaturase and targeted by four sgRNAs. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 and PTG/Cas9 mutagenesis of AcPDS generated indels and, with paired sgRNAs in PTG/Cas9, large chromosomal deletions. 3. Edited AcPDS callus-derived plantlets displayed albino leaves, validating AcPDS disruption as the cause of the pigmentation phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 and PTG/Cas9 targeted mutagenesis; T7E1 assay; Sanger sequencing; regeneration of edited plantlets; phenotype observation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue culture regeneration under 12-h light/12-h dark at 26 °C; G418 selection
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-1 promoter (Pol III, constitutive in construct)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful visible marker gene for establishing and optimizing genome-editing systems in kiwifruit; not a direct nutritional improvement target.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Albino and likely dwarf/defective growth upon loss of function, making it unsuitable for direct crop improvement of nutritional traits.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AcPDS served as a reporter gene to compare editing system efficiency rather than as a nutritional biofortification target; PTG/Cas9 achieved about 10-fold higher editing frequencies than conventional CRISPR/Cas9 in this locus.
